user_prompt: review platformio.ini and `lib\ESP-FTP-Server-Lib\library.json` and `lib\ESP-FTP-Server-Lib\platformio.ini` and make sure this builds right now with no code changes
agent_reply: **Findings**
1. **The default project build does not succeed right now.**

I ran `pio run -e thefly`, which matches the default environment in [platformio.ini](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:2). The build gets through compilation and fails at link time with an IRAM overflow:
```text
.iram0.text will not fit in region iram0_0_seg
IRAM0 segment data does not fit
region iram0_0_seg overflowed by 2920 bytes
So the current PlatformIO/library setup is not a dependency-resolution blocker, but the project still does not produce a firmware image right now.
2. **The FTP library is being picked up from the local `lib/` copy as intended.**

In the tested build, PlatformIO compiled objects from `lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib`, including the FTP server sources. The root [platformio.ini](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:121) no longer lists the registry dependency for `kastenklicker/ESP-FTP-Server`, so the vendored library is the one in play for this build.
3. **The vendored library metadata disagrees with itself.**
[library.json](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/library.json:2) says:
- name: `ESP-FTP-Server`
- version: `0.14.2`
But [library.properties](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/library.properties:1) says:
- name: `ESP-FTP-Server-Lib`
- version: `0.14.1`
That mismatch did **not** stop this PlatformIO build from finding and compiling the library, but it is worth cleaning up later because it makes the library identity/version ambiguous.
4. **The library’s own `platformio.ini` is not the parent build configuration.**

[lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/platformio.ini](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/platformio.ini:1) defines standalone library test/example environments. The parent firmware build uses the root [platformio.ini](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:1), so the vendored library’s `platformio.ini` is not what controls `pio run -e thefly`.
5. **There is a non-fatal FTP library compatibility warning.**

The build warns at [ESP-FTP-Server-Lib.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib.cpp:40) that `NetworkServer::available()` is deprecated and has been renamed to `accept()`. That is not today’s failure, but it is a sign the vendored library is rubbing against the current networking API.
**Assumptions / Notes**
- I reviewed and built without making code changes.
- I treated `thefly` as the build that matters because it is the root config’s `default_envs`.
- The immediate failure is memory placement at link time, not a malformed `library.json` or a bad `lib_deps` entry.
**Summary**

The PlatformIO setup is currently resolving the local `lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib` and compiling it. The build still fails now because `thefly` exceeds IRAM by 2920 bytes. The metadata mismatch between `library.json` and `library.properties` is a cleanup item, but it is not the build-stopper I observed.

user_prompt: to `lib\ESP-FTP-Server-Lib\src` add a public function called `isBusy`. Then add a wrapper around `lib\FtpServer\FtpServer.cpp` to get the `isBusy` of the only single instance of the server, if it has started, otherwise `isBusy` returns false.

`isBusy` is much like `lib\WifiManager\AsyncFsManager.cpp` where it has `AsyncFsManager::guiShouldYield()`, anything that accesses the microSD card will block the update of the GUI, using a lock and other mechanisms
`FlyGui::guiShouldYield()` will start to check `FtpServer::isBusy()` as well
agent_reply: Done.
Added FTP busy plumbing through the local library and wrapper:

- `FTPConnection::isBusy()` in [FTPConnection.h](/c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPConnection.h) / [FTPConnection.cpp](/c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPConnection.cpp)

- `FTPServer::isBusy()` in [ESP-FTP-Server-Lib.h](/c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib.h) / [ESP-FTP-Server-Lib.cpp](/c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib.cpp)
- `FtpServer::isBusy()` wrapper in [FtpServer.h](/c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FtpServer/FtpServer.h) / [FtpServer.cpp](/c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FtpServer/FtpServer.cpp)
`FtpServer::isBusy()` returns false until the singleton server has started. Once started, it reports busy if a connection has pending control input or an active `RETR`/`STOR` transfer.
`FlyGui::guiShouldYield()` now checks both:
```cpp
AsyncFsManager::guiShouldYield() || FtpServer::isBusy()
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`; build passes. There’s still the existing deprecated `NetworkServer::available()` warning inside the FTP library.

user_prompt: I think there's way more places where the connection should be marked busy, anything that risks having a SPI transfer happen, so listing directories and such.
I would have thought `lib\ESP-FTP-Server-Lib\src\FTPFilesystem.cpp` would have been massively changed with something around every function
agent_reply: Yep, you were right. I broadened it.
`FTPFilesystem` now owns a nested RAII `BusyScope`, an `isBusy()` flag, and wraps the filesystem methods that can touch the SD card: `open`, `exists`, `remove`, `rename`, `mkdir`, and `rmdir`.
I also wrapped authenticated FTP command execution in `FTPConnection::handle()` with `FTPFilesystem::BusyScope`, which covers command-local `FsFile` work like:
- `LIST` / `NLST` / `MLSD` directory iteration
- `STAT` directory scanning
- `CWD` directory checks
- `RETR` / `STOR` setup work
- `DELE`, `MKD`, `RMD`, `RNFR` / `RNTO`
`FTPServer::isBusy()` now reports true if either the filesystem busy scope is active or any connection has pending command input / active transfer.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`; build passes. Still has the existing deprecated `NetworkServer::available()` warning.
